text.title: CodeQL + Copilot Autofix Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed when adopting CodeQL + Autofix. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: CodeQL check marked "informational" because team is overwhelmed by onboarding alerts; new alerts merge in regardless.
antipattern.root-cause: Existing baseline of alerts treated as merge blockers; team softened gate.
antipattern.fix: Baseline existing alerts once as "won't fix"; keep the gate hard for every new alert. Required check stays required.

antipattern.symptom: Autofix alerts dismissed in bulk with no reason; audit trail is empty; same alert reappears.
antipattern.root-cause: Dismissals without `--reason` poison the audit trail.
antipattern.fix: Require one of `false-positive` | `won-t-fix` | `used-in-tests` per dismissal; CI lints dismissal records.

antipattern.symptom: Workflow auto-merges Autofix patches; AI-generated patch broke runtime behaviour.
antipattern.root-cause: Workflow misconfigured to bypass human review.
antipattern.fix: Set autofix_auto_merge=false; require human review on every Autofix-authored commit before merge.
